conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions. Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex, who is 10, and Blake, who is 2 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have two children - Alex (10 years old) and Blake (2 years old).

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake, I need to ask you some questions.

First, **do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, there are specific rules that apply.

Could you please tell me **which country you are currently living in?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Living in Spain does affect eligibility, as Child Benefit is generally for people living in the UK, though there are some exceptions.

Could you tell me: **Are you a Crown servant (working for the UK government abroad), or are you or your partner working for a UK employer while in Spain?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant and neither I nor my partner work for a UK employer.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. 

One more question about your residency status: **Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or any other UK immigration status?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and I don't have any other UK immigration status.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one final question to ensure I have a complete picture:

**Are you planning to return to live in the UK, or have you moved to Spain permanently?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: We've moved to Spain permanently. We're not planning to return to the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

Based on the official UK government guidance, I can now provide you with a determination on your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Eligibility Decision

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake.

**For Alex (10 years old):**
While Alex meets the age requirement (under 16), you do not meet the residency requirement. To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally must live in the UK. Since you are living permanently in Spain and are not a Crown servant, and you don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex.

**For Blake (2 years old):**
Similarly, while Blake meets the age requirement (under 16), the same residency issue applies. You do not meet the UK residency requirement necessary to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

**Summary:**
Child Benefit is generally only available to people who live in the UK. The main exceptions are for Crown servants (UK government employees working abroad) or those with specific immigration status like settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Unfortunately, since you've moved to Spain permanently and don't fall into any of these exception categories, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move back to the UK), you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 10 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children, Alex (10) and Blake (2), both of whom meet the age requirements for Child Benefit. However, the claimant is living permanently in Spain and does not meet the residency requirement. To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally must live in the UK. The exceptions for living abroad include being a Crown servant or having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, neither of which apply in this case. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.
